---
title: "SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations)"
author:
  - name: Chris Endemann
    email: endemann@wisc.edu

date: 2026-03-09
date-format: long
image: ../../../images/shap-logo.png

categories:
  - Libraries
  - Explainability
  - Interpretability
  - Trustworthy AI
  - Tabular
  - Computer vision
  - NLP
  - Text analysis
  - Deep learning
  - Sklearn
  - XGBoost
  - LightGBM
  - PyTorch
  - Tensorflow
  - Visualization
---

[SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations)](https://shap.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) is a Python library that uses game-theoretic Shapley values to explain the output of any machine learning model. Developed by [Scott Lundberg](https://github.com/slundberg) and introduced in a [2017 NeurIPS paper](https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper/2017/hash/8a20a8621978632d76c43dfd28b67767-Abstract.html), SHAP provides a unified framework for feature importance and model interpretability. Its mathematically grounded approach assigns each feature an importance value for a given prediction, offering both local (per-prediction) and global (model-wide) explanations.

#### Key features
- **Unified framework**: SHAP unifies several existing explanation methods (LIME, DeepLIFT, etc.) under a single theoretical umbrella based on Shapley values, guaranteeing properties like local accuracy, missingness, and consistency.
- **Multiple explainer types**: Optimized algorithms for different model families:
  - **TreeExplainer**: Fast, exact Shapley value computation for tree-based models (XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost, Random Forest, etc.).
  - **DeepExplainer**: Approximate SHAP values for deep learning models using a modified DeepLIFT algorithm.
  - **GradientExplainer**: Uses expected gradients to explain deep learning model predictions.
  - **KernelExplainer**: Model-agnostic explainer that works with any model by treating it as a black box.
  - **LinearExplainer**: Exact SHAP values for linear models with feature correlation handling.
- **Rich visualizations**: Built-in plots for interpreting model behavior:
  - **Summary plots**: Global feature importance with per-feature impact distributions.
  - **Waterfall plots**: Step-by-step breakdown of a single prediction.
  - **Dependence plots**: Feature interactions and nonlinear effects.
  - **Force plots**: Interactive visualizations of individual or grouped predictions.
  - **Bar plots**: Simple feature importance rankings.
- **Performance**: TreeExplainer computes exact SHAP values in polynomial time, making it practical for production use with tree ensembles.

## Integration and compatibility
SHAP works with virtually any Python ML model, with optimized support for many popular frameworks.

- **Frameworks supported**: scikit-learn, XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost, PyTorch, TensorFlow/Keras, and any model with a predict function
- **Installation instructions**:
  - Basic: `pip install shap`
  - Conda: `conda install -c conda-forge shap`

## Use cases
- **Feature selection**: Identify which features contribute most to model predictions, helping guide feature engineering and dimensionality reduction.
- **Model debugging**: Diagnose unexpected model behavior by examining which features drive specific predictions.
- **Regulatory compliance**: Provide auditable explanations for model decisions in regulated industries (healthcare, finance, lending).
- **Bias detection**: Reveal whether protected attributes (e.g., race, gender) are disproportionately influencing predictions.
- **Model comparison**: Compare feature importance across different model architectures to understand trade-offs.
- **Stakeholder communication**: Generate intuitive visualizations that make model behavior accessible to non-technical audiences.

## Tutorials and resources
- **[Official documentation](https://shap.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)**: Comprehensive API reference and usage guide.
- **[Example notebooks](https://shap.readthedocs.io/en/latest/examples.html)**: Walkthroughs covering tabular data, text, and image explanations.
- **[GitHub repository](https://github.com/shap/shap)**: Source code, issues, and community discussions.
- **[Original paper](https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper/2017/hash/8a20a8621978632d76c43dfd28b67767-Abstract.html)**: "A Unified Approach to Interpreting Model Predictions" (Lundberg & Lee, NeurIPS 2017).

## Related resources
- [**Book**: Interpretable Machine Learning](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Learn/Books/Interpretable-Machine-Learning_ChristophMolnar.html): A comprehensive guide covering SHAP and other model interpretation methods in depth.
- [**Workshop**: Trustworthy AI: Explainability, Bias, Fairness, and Safety](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Learn/Workshops/TrustworthyAI_Explainability-Bias-Fairness-OODdetection.html): Hands-on workshop covering explainability techniques, bias mitigation, and model safety.
- [**Library**: AI Fairness 360 (AIF360)](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Toolbox/Libraries/AI-Fairness-360.html): Complementary library for detecting and mitigating bias in ML models — pair with SHAP for a comprehensive trustworthy AI workflow.

## Comments
